title: Master Institutional Manifesto v1.0
subtitle: Why We Are Building This
purpose: Technical plans explain how. Strategic plans explain what. The manifesto explains why. Moral and philosophical foundation of Citizens United Facts. Read by every volunteer, Education Leader, coalition partner, and future leader before serving. If every other document were lost, this explains the institution's purpose.
governing_principle: Everything we build ultimately answers one question: Will this help an ordinary Arkansan better understand how self-government works? If yes, we build it. If no, we do not. That simple question guides Citizens United Facts long after every founder, volunteer, technology platform, and generation has changed. It is the institution's compass — always pointing toward service.
founders_principle: One day, someone who has never met the founders may log in simply to understand something. They may never know how many volunteers built it, how many conversations shaped it, how many evenings were spent researching, how many communities contributed. That is exactly as it should be. The institution was never built to honor its founders. It was built to serve Arkansas.

sections.title: We Believe
sections.summary: Democracy, trustworthy information, transparency, civic education for everyone
beliefs: Informed citizens require access to trustworthy information
Trustworthy information requires transparency, evidence, humility, and intellectual honesty
Civic education should belong to everyone

sections.title: We Believe Questions Matter
sections.summary: Questions are the beginning of understanding — not weaknesses
example_questions: What does Citizens United actually mean?

sections.title: We Believe Knowledge Should Be Accessible
sections.summary: Complex legal and government topics explained for every Arkansan

sections.title: We Believe Civic Education Should Unite Communities
sections.summary: Learning → conversations → understanding → respect → stronger communities

sections.title: We Believe Arkansas Can Lead
sections.summary: Great civic institutions can begin in Arkansas — built by volunteers, guided by evidence

sections.title: We Believe Volunteers Change History
sections.summary: Institutions built by ordinary Arkansans accomplishing extraordinary things

sections.title: We Believe Trust Must Be Earned
sections.summary: Trust through careful research, transparent sources, respectful dialogue, responsible leadership

sections.title: We Believe Institutions Should Outlive Their Founders
sections.summary: Success measured by continued service to Arkansas decades from now

sections.title: We Believe Education Comes Before Advocacy
sections.summary: Help people understand — Arkansans draw their own conclusions
responsibilities: Distinguish fact from interpretation and policy

sections.title: We Believe Communities Matter More Than Algorithms
sections.summary: Civic education built through relationships — technology supports, does not replace

sections.title: We Believe in Arkansas
sections.summary: Arkansas deserves civic understanding, volunteers, transparent research, thoughtful leadership

our_promise.title: Our Promise
commitments: Protect the public trust entrusted to us

institutional_motto.title: Institutional Motto
institutional_motto.full: Learn with Curiosity. Lead with Integrity. Serve with Humility. Strengthen Arkansas.

integration.chain: Manifesto → Organizational Constitution → Operating Manual → Public Trust Framework → Volunteer Network → Education Academy

long_term_vision: The manifesto remains the compass when technology changes, volunteers rotate, and generations pass. Every decision returns to one question: Does this help an ordinary Arkansan understand self-government? The institution serves Arkansas — not its founders.
catalog_gaps: Required reading not enforced in onboarding
No public-facing manifesto page separate from MC
Onboarding flow does not gate service on manifesto read
Coalition partners not prompted to acknowledge manifesto
Education Leaders no manifesto certification step
Manifesto not linked from volunteer signup funnel
No translation or accessibility review of manifesto
Motto not displayed consistently across public site

recommended_next_build.title: Master Founding Charter (The Arkansas Declaration for an Informed Citizenry)
recommended_next_build.note: Completion of the founding blueprint — permanent charter foundation, founding documents bundle, covenant, January 2027 goals, COMP-* specs.
